methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $15.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $400M - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Dataminr
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series F), post-money $4.1B

data.narrative:
Applied Intuition, a Series F AI & Software company founded by Qasar Younis and Peter Ludwig, carries a last post-money valuation of $15.0B following $1.2B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 11 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, including 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private peers such as xAI, Anthropic, Groq, and SandboxAQ, with valuations ranging from $400M to $250.0B.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement, implying $19.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 11-company comparable set yields $4.1B, though the distribution is wide given the presence of mega-cap outliers (xAI at $250.0B, Anthropic at $183.0B) alongside smaller peers. The median peer valuation of $4.1B reflects structural heterogeneity within the AI & Software sector at this stage.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $3.5B (low) to $22.4B (high), with a median of $11.8B. The $15.0B current valuation sits in the upper-middle portion of this range, consistent with the company's mature Series F status and capital raised. Key uncertainties center on peer comparability—the wide dispersion between mega-cap foundation models (xAI, Anthropic) and infrastructure/application-layer software creates meaningful valuation ambiguity. Sector-specific revenue multiples and path to liquidity would sharpen this range materially.
